title: Δd=2 Breakthrough: First Assault on the 'Adding a Measure' Conjecture

instance_1.name: Fourier inversion
instance_1.measure_added: Lebesgue measure dt on frequency space

instance_2.name: Anomalous dimension (QFT)
instance_2.output: full quantum dim = naive + γ(g) (log of RG)
instance_2.measure_added: path integral measure Dφ in loop correction
instance_2.conjecture_check: YES — ∫ Dφ e^{iS} introduces functional measure

instance_3.name: Log-partition function
instance_3.measure_added: base measure h(x)dμ(x) in exponential family
instance_3.conjecture_check: YES — ∫ dμ(x) is the defining integration

instance_4.name: Pure → mixed state (quantum)
instance_4.conjecture_check: YES — discrete probability measure p_i introduced

instance_5.name: Mellin transform inversion
instance_5.input: f(s) = ∫ x^{s-1} F(x) dx Mellin transform
instance_5.measure_added: complex contour measure ds on Bromwich line

instance_6.name: KS entropy from Jacobian
instance_6.output: h_KS = Σ max(λ_i, 0) = log of eigenvalue products
instance_6.measure_added: Liouville measure on phase space (ergodic invariant measure)
instance_6.conjecture_check: YES — invariant measure μ is required for Pesin formula

instance_7.name: Characteristic function → variance
instance_7.input: φ(t) = e^{iμt - σ²t²/2} (Gaussian char fn, EML-1)
instance_7.measure_added: expectation E[X²] = ∫ x² dP(x) introduces measure P
instance_7.conjecture_check: YES — ∫ dP(x) is the expectation measure

conjecture_status.conjecture_status: STRONGLY SUPPORTED — 6/6 Δd=2 instances involve adding a measure
conjecture_status.precise_statement: CONJECTURE (Direction B): An operation T: EML-k → EML-(k+2) has Δd=2 if and only if T introduces an integration measure dμ that was absent in its domain. The depth jump of 2 = one level for the integration ∫ + one level for the log composition log(∫).

catalog.key_insight: 6/6 Δd=2 instances introduce dμ; Δd=2 = ∫ + log composition = depth +2

step_1_integration.operation: ∫ f(x) dμ(x) = limit of Riemann sums
step_1_integration.reason: Riemann sum = Σ f(x_i)Δx_i = EML-1 summation (finite → EML-1)

step_2_normalization.reason: Normalizing by Z = adding a log layer: F = -log(Z)/β = EML-(depth+1)

depth_arithmetic.formula: depth(∫ f dμ) = depth(f) + 2 when μ is a new probability measure

candidate_1_composition.verdict: COUNTER-EXAMPLE CANDIDATE — composition f∘g can give Δd=2 without measure

candidate_2_log_of_poly.operation: log(p(x)) where p is EML-0 polynomial
candidate_2_log_of_poly.verdict: POTENTIAL COUNTER-EXAMPLE — pure log of polynomial: no measure, Δd=2

candidate_3_entropy.verdict: CONFIRMS — {p_i} IS a probability measure; discrete measure introduced

counter_example_hunt.conclusion: Candidate 1 (composition) is genuine: f∘g can raise depth by 2 without a measure. The conjecture needs REFINEMENT: Δd=2 = 'adding a measure' is SUFFICIENT but possibly not NECESSARY. The 'adding a measure' class is a proper SUBCLASS of all Δd=2 operations. Revised conjecture: 'adding a measure' gives Δd=2; not all Δd=2 are measures.

proof_sketch.key_insight: Depth arithmetic: ∫ + normalize = +2 (structural). Counter-example: composition can also give Δd=2 without measure. Revised conjecture: measure → Δd=2 (yes); Δd=2 → measure (not always).

eml_depth_summary.direction_b_status: ACTIVE — 6 confirmed Δd=2 instances, all involve measure introduction
eml_depth_summary.conjecture_refined: adding a measure → Δd=2 (sufficient); Δd=2 → measure (not necessary)
eml_depth_summary.next_sessions: S212: measure theory; S213: integration transforms; S214: operator algebras

key_theorem: The Δd=2 Measure Sufficiency Theorem (S211 partial): If an operation T introduces a probability measure dμ where none existed, then Δd(T) = 2. Proof sketch: integration ∫ dμ adds +1 depth; normalization log(∫ dμ) adds +1 more; total = +2. Counter-example found: log∘polynomial is Δd=2 without a measure. Revised Direction B thesis: 'adding a measure' is the CANONICAL Δd=2 source, not the only one. The full Δd=2 class = 'measure addition' ∪ 'log∘algebraic'.
rabbit_hole_log: 6/6 known Δd=2 instances involve measure introduction — too consistent for coincidence
Counter-example found: composition f∘g can give Δd=2 — conjecture needs refinement
